briefing.headline: Rig up a Texas Rig — aggressive summer approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Texas Rig is a strong choice for summer with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: In mornings bass sit looser to the outside of cover; as the sun gets up they move further into the cover, requiring a toad early and flipping deeper in the cover later.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In mornings bass sit looser to the outside of cover; as the sun gets up they move further into the cover, requiring a toad early and flipping deeper in the cover later.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, dock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: DeFronzo: Crimp a small split shot 18 inches above a size 1 hook with a 3-inch Ika Texas-rigged; keep a slow steady retrieve; effective up to 20ft — deeper than that you lose contact; add a grub guard for bottom snags and weed penetration; a glass...

Johnston: On the Potomac, I found fish out in the grass, not in the real thick stuff. I was casting to catch them, not flipping. When that failed due to muddy water from rain, I moved to flipping grass on the Greenway Flats.

KVD: For pitching into grass holes — 3/8oz tungsten weight with a 3/0 Mustad grip pin hook (the pin keeps the bait from sliding down). 5-inch Ocho worm. Blue craw or green pumpkin. Making it fall straight is critical — the Texas rig glides down cl...

Wheeler: The Punch Out Craw has five manifestations - full glide bait on 4/0 hook and 1/2-oz sinker, separated claws for football jig or Biffle Hardhead, trimmed small craw for swim jig/flipping jig/chatterbait/shakyhead, shortened torso for even ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal summer bass feed aggressively. Fish split between shallow morning patterns (2-6ft) and offshore structure (12-25ft). Target grass lines, points, and ledges as falling pressure triggers movement.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
